label: AI Security Specialist
description: Secures AI and machine learning systems covering LLM red teaming, prompt injection, AI governance and risk management, adversarial machine learning, and AI supply chain security.

paths.name: AI Red Team Track
paths.rationale: For offensive practitioners pivoting into AI security. Starts with SecOps Group's practical AI/ML pentesting cert as a hands-on foundation, adds HTB COAE (Google co-developed, launched April 2026) for advanced LLM and agentic AI exploitation, then OSAI/OSAI+ for OffSec brand recognition. This is the most technically grounded AI offensive path available in 2026.
pros: All three are hands-on practical exams — no MCQ-only credentials
Covers the full AI attack surface: LLM, ML models, agentic AI, and AI-integrated apps
HTB COAE co-developed with Google — strong institutional credibility
OSAI brand recognition for hiring managers familiar with OffSec
cons: All certs launched 2025-2026 — HR recognition still building across the board
No DoD 8140 approved AI offensive cert exists yet
Landscape changes fast — recommend re-evaluating every 6 months
OSAI exam availability limited until July 2026

prerequisites.experience: Prior offensive security experience (OSCP equivalent or pentest background) strongly recommended. Python scripting and API familiarity required.
knowledge: LLM fundamentals and prompt engineering basics

cert_sequence.notes: SecOps Group Certified AI/ML Pentester — hands-on practical exam covering OWASP LLM Top 10, prompt injection, model extraction, and adversarial ML. £250 (~$320) with frequent 75% discount codes. The most accessible entry point for offensive practitioners new to AI security. Sets the technical vocabulary and attack framework before COAE's more advanced content.

HTB Certified Offensive AI Expert — co-developed with Google, launched April 2, 2026. Covers advanced LLM exploitation, agentic AI attacks, RAG poisoning, AI supply chain abuse, and multi-modal model vulnerabilities. 100% practical exam with commercial report requirement. The most technically rigorous AI offensive cert currently available. Complete the HTB AI Red Teamer path before attempting — it maps directly to exam content.

OffSec AI Red Teamer (OSAI/OSAI+) — launched March 31, 2026. OffSec brand recognition is the primary value here — hiring managers familiar with OSCP will recognize the OffSec name. OSAI is lifetime; OSAI+ requires a higher pass threshold and 3-year renewal (same model as OSCP/OSCP+). Exam availability opens July 15, 2026. With COAE completed the OSAI content will feel familiar — focus on OffSec's specific methodology and reporting format.

paths.name: AI Security Engineer Track
paths.rationale: For security engineers and defenders building AI security programs. CAISP provides hands-on AI security controls validation, SecAI+ covers enterprise AI security architecture, and GMLE adds ML-specific technical depth. This track prioritizes practical defensibility over governance theory.
pros: CAISP is 100% practical — validates ability to implement AI security controls
Strong combination of technical depth (GMLE) and architectural coverage (SecAI+)
More affordable than the governance track
Transferable to both product security and enterprise AI security roles
cons: All certs are recent — employer recognition still developing
GMLE requires significant ML technical background
No DoD 8140 approved defensive AI cert exists yet
Field evolves rapidly — expect to retrain frequently regardless of certs

prerequisites.experience: Security engineering background recommended. Python or ML framework familiarity (TensorFlow, PyTorch) beneficial for GMLE.

cert_sequence.notes: Certified AI Security Professional — 100% practical exam covering AI system security assessment, secure ML pipeline design, LLM security architecture, and AI-specific threat modeling. The most hands-on AI defensive cert available. Establishes the practitioner foundation before GMLE's deeper ML technical content.

SecAI+ — covers enterprise AI security architecture, AI risk management frameworks, and securing AI-integrated applications. Bridges the gap between hands-on security controls (CAISP) and the governance layer. Good for practitioners whose scope includes advising on AI security architecture decisions.

GIAC Machine Learning Engineer — covers adversarial ML, model security, data poisoning detection, and secure ML pipeline design from a SANS technical perspective. Open-book exam — build indexed notes organized by ML attack category and defensive control. The GIAC brand adds credibility in enterprise and government hiring contexts. Employer-funded if possible given SANS pricing (~$9,800 all-in with SEC595 training).

paths.name: AI Governance & Audit Track
paths.rationale: For GRC professionals, auditors, and compliance officers moving into AI governance. ISACA AAIA is the structured governance credential for existing CISA holders. AIGP from IAPP covers AI policy and regulatory compliance. ISO 42001 Lead Implementer adds the management system framework now required by EU AI Act compliance programs.
pros: AAIA + AIGP + ISO 42001 LI is the most complete AI governance credential stack available
AIGP leverages IAPP's established privacy practitioner network
ISO 42001 directly relevant to EU AI Act compliance — early mover advantage
Accessible to non-technical professionals — no offensive or engineering background required
cons: AAIA requires active CISA — limits entry for non-auditors
High total cost — especially ISO 42001 LI training fees
MCQ-heavy across all three — limited practical depth
EU AI Act compliance timelines may shift, affecting ISO 42001 demand

prerequisites.experience: GRC or audit background strongly recommended. AAIA requires active CISA certification.

cert_sequence.notes: IAPP AI Governance Professional — the most established AI governance credential, from the same organization that runs CIPP/E and CIPM. Covers AI risk management, EU AI Act, algorithmic accountability, and responsible AI frameworks. IAPP's established privacy practitioner network gives AIGP broader reach than newer AI-specific certs. Start here if you have an IAPP background — the CIPP/CIPM skill transfer is significant.

ISACA Auditing AI — structured AI audit methodology for existing CISA holders. Covers AI system audit scope, control assessment for AI decision systems, bias and fairness auditing, and AI-specific risk evaluation frameworks. ISACA's established audit practitioner base gives AAIA strong recognition in enterprise governance contexts. Requires active CISA — if you don't hold CISA, skip to ISO 42001 LI instead.

ISO 42001 Lead Implementer — the emerging international standard for AI management systems, analogous to ISO 27001 for information security. As organizations face EU AI Act pressure, ISO 42001 provides the structured implementation framework that boards and regulators expect. Early expertise in ISO 42001 implementation is a significant differentiator — most compliance teams are just starting to build this capability. PECB delivery is the primary route; training costs are the main financial barrier.
